intro.subtitle: Inspect, materialize, and edit assets without leaving the graph

segments.text: Hey, let's do a super quick tour of Bruin Web. We'll start by opening the customers asset from the sidebar so you can see the editor and the graph together.
If you just want a fast preview, hit Inspect. Bruin runs the asset query and shows the rows right in the results panel.
And right down here, you can see the results panel update without leaving the workspace, so you keep the graph, editor, and data preview in one place.
Once the result looks good, you can materialize the asset. That creates a run entry and keeps the output history handy while you work.
And because Bruin Web is working directly against your workspace files, tiny edits feel really direct. Let's rename the asset so you can see that update happen live.
Even after a reload, the renamed asset is still there. That's the basic loop: browse the graph, inspect data, materialize it, and edit assets in place.
